Caption: Diagram of the experimental set-up for ultrasound-assisted adsorption/desorption of blueberry anthocyanins using macroporous resins. 1: Generator; 2: Electro-thermostatic Water Cabinet; 3: Ultrasonic probe; 4: Conical flask wrapped up in tinfoil containing blueberry extracts and resins; 5: Propeller; 6: Water.
Adsorption kinetic curves of total anthocyanins under different AED levels at 20 °C (a) and 30 °C (b).
Changes of contents of monomeric anthocyanins during ultrasound-assisted adsorption process at 106 (a), 199 (b) and 279 (c) W/L.
FTIR spectrum of macroporous resins after ultrasound-assisted adsorption (a) and desorption (b).
Particle size distribution of macroporous resins before and after ultrasound-assisted adsorption.

Scanning electron micrographs (500 × magnification) for resins before and after ultrasound-assisted adsorption. a: control resins. b: resins treated at 106 W/L; c: resins treated at 199 W/L; d: resins treated at 279 W/L.
Desorption kinetic curves of total anthocyanins under different AED levels at 20 °C (a) and 30 °C (b).
Change of contents of monomeric anthcoyanins during ultrasound-assisted desorption process at 106 (a), 199 (b) and 279 (c) W/L.
